title: Deep Structure of the Continental Plate in the South-Central Chilean Margin: Metamorphic Wedge and Implications for Megathrust Earthquakes
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: Interpretation of 2D seismic velocity models and analysis of seismic waves reflected in the deep zone of the continental crust are presented along the south-central Chilean subduction zone (36°S–41°S). This zone includes the southern portion of the Mw8.8 Maule earthquake (2010) and the northern patch of the Mw9.6 Valdivia earthquake (1960). A variation of seismic structure along the margin is interpreted in terms of the distribution of Permo-Triassic metamorphic units. The change in observed deep structural style suggests differences in frictional properties between the tectonic plates in the zones where the Maule and Valdivia earthquakes ruptured.

location.description: South-central Chilean margin 36°–41°S encompassing both Maule (2010) and Valdivia (1960) earthquake rupture zones

acquisition.vessel: R/V Marcus G. Langseth (MGL1701, CEVICHE 2017) and legacy GEOMAR OBS cruises

data.name: MGL1701 CEVICHE Long-Offset MCS Data — South-Central Chile 36°–41°S
data.description: MGL1701 CEVICHE 15.15-km streamer MCS data used for deep reflection analysis of the continental crust at 36°–41°S; images metamorphic wedge (Permo-Triassic) and deep crustal reflectors

data.name: Published OBS/OBH Velocity Models — South-Central Chile 36°–41°S
data.description: Published 2D P-wave velocity models from SPOC, TIPTEQ and other OBS cruises used to constrain the deep structural interpretation of the continental plate

_data_note: JGR paper; behind paywall. MGL1701 data at MGDS. Also uses published OBS velocity models from SPOC/TIPTEQ.

processing.summary: Interpretation of 2D seismic velocity models (from published OBS data) combined with deep reflection analysis of MGL1701 CEVICHE long-offset seismic data documents the deep structure of the south-central Chilean continental plate (36°–41°S). A Permo-Triassic metamorphic wedge is identified that controls frictional properties and helps explain the different rupture extents of the Maule (2010) and Valdivia (1960) earthquakes.
workflow: 2. Deep reflection analysis (PmP, reflections from 20+ km)
3. Integration with published OBS velocity models
processing.notes: JGR paper using MGL1701 CEVICHE data; companion to Bangs et al. 2020 and Arnulf et al. 2022

analysis_notes: JGR paper; behind paywall. MGL1701 CEVICHE data at MGDS and RVData.
